title: Key concepts: Medieval Muslim Societies
description: Check your understanding of medieval Muslim societies!
topic_path: Social institutions in the Islamic world

choices.content: Non-Muslims who comprised a protected class provided they paid a special tax
Non-Arab Muslims, who were accorded lower status during the Umayyad caliphate
A special set of tax regulations imposed on non-Muslim groups in return for protection
A professional military class, comprised of foreign slave-soldiers
A peasant class that had to pay taxes to a landowner

hints: The Abbasid social hierarchy differed from that of their Umayyad predecessors

choices.content: Gave preference to the Arab elite, and used Arabic as its official administrative language
Was heavily influenced by Persian culture
Ruled over a vast, centralized empire until it was brought down by Mongol invasions

hints: Muslim women were a diverse group

choices.content: Were able to move freely through markets, mosques, and other public spaces
Led very different lives from one another, depending on their class and the political context

hints: This was something given by the government to individuals

choices.content: A land grant, often given to soldiers as a form of reimbursement
A rent that peasants had to pay to their landowner
A centrally organized system of taxation
